entities.name: HIMACHAL PRADESH
entities.description: A northern state of India situated to the south of the Himalaya.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: Country where Himachal Pradesh is located.

entities.name: HIMALAYA
entities.description: Mountain range north of Himachal Pradesh.

entities.name: OFF-SEASON VEGETABLES
entities.description: Vegetables produced in Himachal Pradesh due to conducive agro-climatic conditions.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL CONTROL
entities.description: The use of living organisms to manage pests as an alternative to chemical insecticides.

entities.name: ENTOMOPATHOGENIC NEMATODES
entities.description: Nematodes that parasitize insects, used for biological control.

entities.name: TURNIP MOTH
entities.description: Agrotis segetum, a lepidopteran pest targeted for control.

entities.name: INSECTICIDES
entities.description: Chemical substances used to kill insects, which the study aims to minimize.

entities.name: CROP PROTECTION
entities.description: The practice of managing pests and diseases to protect crops.

entities.name: HETERORHABDITIS INDICA
entities.description: A species of EPN found to be highly effective against turnip moth larvae.

entities.name: HETERORHABDITIS BACTERIOPHORA
entities.description: A species of EPN (isolate HRJ) effective against turnip moth larvae.

entities.name: HETERORHABDITIS SP HSG
entities.description: A local isolate of Heterorhabditis sp. effective against turnip moth.

entities.name: HETERORHABDITIS SP HKM
entities.description: A local isolate of Heterorhabditis sp. effective against turnip moth.

entities.name: SOIL BIOASSAY
entities.description: A laboratory or field test conducted in soil to evaluate the efficacy of EPNs.

entities.name: FIELD AND GREENHOUSE CONDITIONS
entities.description: The environmental settings where EPNs can be applied to manage turnip moth.
